brief: Build a Python CLI tool csv2json: cli.py reads a CSV file path argument and prints a JSON array of row objects to stdout; a --pretty flag produces indented output; quoted fields containing commas and missing values must be handled correctly; include a pytest test suite under tests/ (verify with python3 -m pytest if available, else run cli.py directly on sample files); include a README.md with usage examples. Use only the Python standard library.

tasks.description: Create `cli.py` using only Python standard library modules (`argparse`, `csv`, `json`). It must accept a CSV file path as a positional argument, parse rows into dictionaries using headers, and output a JSON array to stdout. Implement the `--pretty` flag for indented JSON output. Ensure robust handling of quoted fields containing commas (preserving them in values) and missing values (converting empty/missing cells or columns to empty strings `""` in JSON).

assertions.statement: The CLI runs successfully on a valid CSV file and exits with code 0.
Quoted fields containing commas are parsed correctly as a single value in the JSON output.
assertions.command: printf 'name,age\n"Smith, John",30\n' > /tmp/t2.csv && python3 cli.py /tmp/t2.csv | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); assert d[0]['name'] == 'Smith, John'"

assertions.statement: Missing values (empty cells or missing columns) are converted to empty strings in the JSON output.
assertions.command:
printf 'a,b
1,
2
' > /tmp/t3.csv && python3 cli.py /tmp/t3.csv | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); assert d[0]['b'] == '' and d[1]['b'] == ''"

assertions.statement: The --pretty flag produces indented JSON output.
assertions.command:
printf 'a
1
' > /tmp/t4.csv && python3 cli.py --pretty /tmp/t4.csv | python3 -c "import sys; s=sys.stdin.read(); assert '
' in s and '  ' in s"

tasks.description: Develop a `pytest`-compatible test suite under `tests/test_cli.py`. Include tests for basic conversion, quoted commas, missing values, and the `--pretty` flag. Provide sample CSV files to allow direct CLI verification if `pytest` is unavailable, satisfying the fallback verification requirement.

assertions.statement: printf 'a,b\n1,2\n' > /tmp/t2_sample.csv && (python3 -m pytest tests/ --tb=short > /dev/null 2>&1 || python3 cli.py /tmp/t2_sample.csv > /dev/null 2>&1); rm -f /tmp/t2_sample.csv; echo $?
printf 'col1,col2\n"a,b",c\n' > tests/sample_quoted.csv && python3 cli.py tests/sample_quoted.csv | grep -q '"b"'; rm -f tests/sample_quoted.csv; echo $?

tasks.description: Draft a comprehensive `README.md` covering installation, usage examples, flag descriptions (specifically `--pretty`), and notes on CSV parsing behavior. Include instructions for running the test suite via `pytest` or direct CLI execution.

assertions.statement: [ -f README.md ] && grep -qiE '(usage|example)' README.md && grep -q -- '--pretty' README.md; echo $?

items.command: printf 'name,age\n"Smith, John",30\n' > /tmp/t2.csv && python3 cli.py /tmp/t2.csv | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); assert d[0]['name'] == 'Smith, John'"

printf 'a,b
1,
2
' > /tmp/t3.csv && python3 cli.py /tmp/t3.csv | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); assert d[0]['b'] == '' and d[1]['b'] == '', f'd[0][b]={repr(d[0].get("b"))}, d[1][b]={repr(d[1].get("b"))}'; print('PASS')"

printf 'a
1
' > /tmp/t4.csv && python3 cli.py --pretty /tmp/t4.csv | python3 -c "import sys; s=sys.stdin.read(); assert '
' in s and '  ' in s, f'newline={chr(10) in s}, indent={"  " in s}'; print('PASS')"
printf 'a,b\n1,2\n' > /tmp/t2_sample.csv && (python3 -m pytest tests/ --tb=short > /dev/null 2>&1 || python3 cli.py /tmp/t2_sample.csv > /dev/null 2>&1); rm -f /tmp/t2_sample.csv; echo $?
printf 'col1,col2\n"a,b",c\n' > tests/sample_quoted.csv && python3 cli.py tests/sample_quoted.csv | grep -q '"b"'; rm -f tests/sample_quoted.csv; echo $?
[ -f README.md ] && grep -qiE '(usage|example)' README.md && grep -q -- '--pretty' README.md; echo $?
